sources.notes: Harder interactive version with different k,c constraints.
Writeup derives fixed query bases 36, 3239, and 100003 and resolves residual candidate sets.

annotation.summary: Interactively guess a hidden integer from digit-sum responses in carefully chosen bases.
annotation.constraints: The query budget is tiny, so the strategy must precompute a compact decision tree over residues and threshold replies.
annotation.core_idea: A query in base b returns -1 if x<b, otherwise a digit sum congruent to x modulo b-1. Use fixed bases whose moduli split the range into manageable residue classes; if a -1 reply shrinks the range, query a smaller base and resolve the remaining candidate set by checking digit-sum signatures.
annotation.complexity: O(1) interactive queries with small offline candidate filtering.

solution_variants.name: fixed-bases-decision-tree
solution_variants.summary: Use several preselected bases and branch on -1 replies to keep a unique candidate.

tags.evidence: The solution is a bounded-query interactive protocol.
Large-case reconstruction combines residues modulo selected b-1 values.
The hard version depends on branching over -1 answers and resolving candidate sets.
tags.description: Design a small adaptive branching strategy where each response narrows a finite candidate set.
tags.created_reason: Interactive and information-theoretic problems often hinge on decision-tree construction.

tags.evidence: Threshold replies act like comparison information, but the main solution is CRT plus a fixed decision tree.
